interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce renal clearance of methotrexate, increasing plasma levels.
interactions.consequence: Methotrexate toxicity: bone marrow suppression, hepatotoxicity, mucositis.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid concurrent use; if unavoidable, reduce MTX dose and monitor LFTs weekly.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use paracetamol for analgesia in patients on methotrexate.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit prostaglandin-mediated renal blood flow, reducing MTX clearance.
interactions.consequence: Elevated MTX levels → severe myelosuppression and nephrotoxicity.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid concurrent use.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Paracetamol or short-course corticosteroids preferred.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs inhibit dihydrofolate reductase; additive antifolate effect.
interactions.temporal_flag: Contraindicated combination.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use alternative antibiotics (e.g., amoxicillin) if infection requires treatment.

interactions.mechanism: Pharmacodynamic synergy: dual anticoagulation + platelet inhibition.
interactions.consequence: Significantly increased risk of major bleeding including GI and intracranial haemorrhage.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid unless benefit clearly outweighs risk (e.g., mechanical heart valve).
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use gastroprotection with PPI if combination is clinically necessary.

interactions.mechanism: Metronidazole inhibits CYP2C9, reducing warfarin metabolism and increasing INR.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor INR closely; consider warfarin dose reduction by 25–50%.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use azithromycin or doxycycline for anaerobic infections where applicable.

interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 and CYP2C9 metabolism of warfarin.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor INR after 2–3 days of antibiotic initiation.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use amoxicillin-clavulanate if spectrum covers the indication.

interactions.mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19, reducing conversion of clopidogrel to active metabolite.
interactions.consequence: Reduced antiplatelet effect; increased risk of cardiovascular events.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination; use pantoprazole or rabeprazole as PPI alternative.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Pantoprazole has lower CYP2C19 inhibition and is preferred.

interactions.mechanism: Contrast-induced nephropathy reduces metformin excretion.
interactions.temporal_flag: Hold metformin 48 hours before and after iodinated contrast administration.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Resume only after confirming stable renal function post-procedure.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and CYP3A4, increasing digoxin levels.
interactions.consequence: Digoxin toxicity: bradycardia, AV block, arrhythmias.
interactions.temporal_flag: Reduce digoxin dose by 50% when starting amiodarone; monitor levels.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use bisoprolol for rate control if digoxin toxicity risk is high.

interactions.mechanism: Furosemide-induced hypokalaemia potentiates digoxin toxicity.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor potassium levels; supplement potassium as needed.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Add spironolactone to maintain potassium balance.

interactions.mechanism: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase, increasing toxic azathioprine metabolites.
interactions.consequence: Severe myelosuppression and bone marrow failure.
interactions.temporal_flag: Contraindicated. If both required, reduce azathioprine dose by 75%.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use febuxostat (lower interaction) or switch to mycophenolate.

interactions.mechanism: Additive hyperkalaemia from dual renin-angiotensin-aldosterone blockade.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor serum potassium within 1 week of combination initiation.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use loop diuretics (furosemide) instead of potassium-sparing agents if possible.

interactions.mechanism: Both reduce potassium excretion via different mechanisms; additive effect.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor electrolytes weekly for first month.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use furosemide for diuresis if hyperkalaemia develops.

interactions.mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2, markedly increasing theophylline levels.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor theophylline levels; consider dose reduction by 30–50%.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use azithromycin or amoxicillin-clavulanate as antibiotics in patients on theophylline.

interactions.mechanism: HCQ increases digoxin plasma levels via P-gp inhibition.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor digoxin levels when starting or stopping HCQ.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use bisoprolol for rate control in patients on HCQ.

interactions.mechanism: Corticosteroids cause hyperglycaemia, blunting metformin effect.
interactions.consequence: Loss of glycaemic control; steroid-induced diabetes.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor blood glucose; adjust antidiabetic therapy during steroid course.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Add basal insulin if glucose cannot be controlled with oral agents alone.

interactions.mechanism: Both agents independently damage gastric mucosa.
interactions.consequence: Significantly increased risk of peptic ulcer and GI bleeding.
interactions.temporal_flag: Co-prescribe PPI (pantoprazole 40 mg OD) for gastroprotection.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Prefer paracetamol for analgesia over NSAIDs in patients on steroids.

interactions.mechanism: Alcohol inhibits gluconeogenesis and increases lactic acid production.
interactions.temporal_flag: Advise patients to avoid excessive alcohol consumption.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Counsel patient on alcohol restriction.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium chelates levothyroxine in the GI tract, reducing absorption.
interactions.temporal_flag: Separate doses by at least 4 hours.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Take levothyroxine on empty stomach, calcium supplement after meals.

interactions.mechanism: Iron chelates levothyroxine, reducing oral absorption.
interactions.temporal_flag: Separate administration by at least 4 hours.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Administer levothyroxine on waking, iron with meals.

interactions.mechanism: Phenytoin initially inhibits, then induces CYP2C9 metabolism of warfarin; biphasic effect.
interactions.consequence: Initial INR rise then fall; unpredictable anticoagulation.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor INR frequently when initiating or discontinuing phenytoin.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use levetiracetam (no CYP interactions) in anticoagulated patients.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP2C9, substantially increasing warfarin effect.
interactions.temporal_flag: Reduce warfarin dose by 30–50% when starting amiodarone; monitor INR weekly.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Consider DOAC (rivaroxaban/dabigatran) if amiodarone is long-term.

interactions.mechanism: Carbamazepine induces CYP3A4, accelerating oestrogen and progestogen metabolism.
interactions.temporal_flag: Use alternative contraception (IUCD, barrier methods).
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Switch to lamotrigine or levetiracetam if epilepsy control is adequate.

interactions.mechanism: NSAIDs reduce prostaglandin-mediated renal lithium excretion.
interactions.consequence: Lithium toxicity: tremor, confusion, renal failure.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid NSAIDs in patients on lithium; monitor lithium levels if unavoidable.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use paracetamol for analgesia.

interactions.mechanism: Additive nitric oxide-mediated vasodilation causing profound hypotension.
interactions.temporal_flag: Absolute contraindication. Do not combine.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use amlodipine for angina in patients using PDE-5 inhibitors.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, substantially increasing atorvastatin levels.
interactions.temporal_flag: Hold atorvastatin during clarithromycin course, or switch to pravastatin.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Pravastatin and rosuvastatin are not CYP3A4 substrates and are safer alternatives.

interactions.mechanism: Amiodarone inhibits CYP3A4, raising simvastatin levels.
interactions.temporal_flag: Limit simvastatin to 20 mg/day; prefer rosuvastatin.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Switch to rosuvastatin or pravastatin.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs prolong QTc interval; additive cardiac risk.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination; monitor ECG if unavoidable.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use doxycycline or amoxicillin instead of azithromycin.

interactions.mechanism: Additive diuresis leading to volume depletion.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor blood pressure, renal function, and hydration status.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Reduce furosemide dose when initiating SGLT2 inhibitor.

interactions.mechanism: Complementary glucose-lowering mechanisms; no pharmacokinetic interaction.
interactions.consequence: Risk of hypoglycaemia if sulphonylurea also co-prescribed.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor glucose; note that SGLT2i alone rarely cause hypoglycaemia.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Safe combination — consider reducing sulphonylurea if co-prescribed.

interactions.mechanism: Dual antiplatelet therapy: additive bleeding risk.
interactions.consequence: Significantly increased risk of GI bleeding.
interactions.temporal_flag: Co-prescribe PPI (pantoprazole preferred over omeprazole).
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Duration of dual antiplatelet therapy should follow ACS/PCI guidelines.

interactions.mechanism: Additive anticoagulant + antiplatelet effect increases bleeding risk.
interactions.temporal_flag: Limit to evidence-based indications (e.g., COMPASS trial doses).
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use gastroprotection; reassess dual therapy indication regularly.

interactions.mechanism: DPP-4 inhibitors increase bradykinin levels, potentiating ACE inhibitor-induced cough.
interactions.temporal_flag: If cough worsens, consider switching ACE inhibitor to ARB (telmisartan/losartan).
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Telmisartan does not affect bradykinin pathway.

interactions.mechanism: Clarithromycin inhibits P-gp and CYP3A4, substantially increasing colchicine exposure.
interactions.consequence: Colchicine toxicity: multi-organ failure, neuromuscular toxicity.
interactions.temporal_flag: Reduce colchicine dose by 50% or avoid combination.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use alternative antibiotic; if unavoidable, hold colchicine during antibiotic course.

interactions.mechanism: Additive calcineurin inhibition; pharmacokinetic interactions via P-gp and CYP3A4.
interactions.temporal_flag: Contraindicated combination in most protocols.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use monotherapy with therapeutic drug monitoring.

interactions.mechanism: Cyclosporine inhibits OATP1B1 and CYP3A4, markedly increasing statin exposure.
interactions.temporal_flag: Use lowest possible statin dose; avoid high-dose statins.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Pravastatin is preferred (minimal CYP metabolism) in transplant patients.

interactions.mechanism: Additive hepatotoxicity.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor LFTs monthly; avoid in patients with pre-existing liver disease.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Combination used in refractory RA but requires close LFT monitoring.

interactions.mechanism: Valproate displaces phenytoin from protein binding and inhibits its metabolism.
interactions.consequence: Phenytoin toxicity (nystagmus, ataxia) despite normal total levels.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor free phenytoin levels, not total phenytoin.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Consider alternative AED combinations with neurologist guidance.

interactions.mechanism: Additive CNS depression.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination if possible; use minimum effective doses.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use SSRI for anxiety in patients requiring pregabalin for neuropathic pain.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs lower seizure threshold; additive serotonergic effect.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use paracetamol or NSAIDs for pain in patients on amitriptyline.

interactions.mechanism: Additive serotonergic activity.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination; use non-opioid analgesia.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Paracetamol or NSAIDs preferred for pain.

interactions.mechanism: Both drugs prolong QTc; additive cardiac risk.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid if possible; ECG monitoring mandatory if combined.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use amoxicillin or doxycycline instead of azithromycin.

interactions.mechanism: Olanzapine causes metabolic syndrome, insulin resistance, and weight gain.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor HbA1c and fasting glucose every 3 months.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Aripiprazole has lower metabolic liability; discuss with psychiatrist.

interactions.mechanism: Rifampicin is a potent CYP2C9 inducer, greatly accelerating warfarin metabolism.
interactions.temporal_flag: Increase warfarin dose substantially; monitor INR every 3–5 days until stable.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use DOAC if TB treatment requires rifampicin (note: some DOACs also interact).

interactions.mechanism: Rifampicin induces CYP enzymes and increases glucuronidation of T4.
interactions.temporal_flag: Increase levothyroxine dose; monitor TSH monthly during TB treatment.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Resume pre-treatment dose when rifampicin is stopped.

interactions.mechanism: Divalent calcium ions chelate doxycycline, reducing oral absorption by up to 80%.
interactions.temporal_flag: Separate administration by at least 2–3 hours.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Take doxycycline on empty stomach; administer calcium after meals.

interactions.mechanism: Calcium chelates ciprofloxacin reducing absorption.
interactions.temporal_flag: Separate by at least 2 hours.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Administer ciprofloxacin 2 hours before calcium supplement.

interactions.mechanism: Furosemide increases metformin plasma levels by reducing renal tubular secretion.
interactions.consequence: Increased metformin levels; lactic acidosis risk in renal impairment.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor renal function; adjust metformin dose in deteriorating kidney function.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Hold metformin if eGFR falls below 30 mL/min/1.73 m².

interactions.mechanism: Dual RAAS blockade: additive hyperkalaemia, hypotension, and renal impairment.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination (ONTARGET trial evidence).
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use single RAAS agent at optimal dose.

interactions.mechanism: Pharmacokinetic interaction: morphine increases gabapentin absorption.
interactions.temporal_flag: Use lowest effective doses; monitor respiratory rate.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Consider non-opioid pain adjuncts (duloxetine, amitriptyline) for neuropathic pain.

interactions.mechanism: Allopurinol increases risk of ampicillin-induced skin rash.
interactions.temporal_flag: Monitor for rash; usually not contraindicated but awareness required.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use cephalosporins if rash is a clinical concern.

interactions.mechanism: Valproate inhibits glucuronidation of lamotrigine, doubling its half-life.
interactions.temporal_flag: Reduce lamotrigine dose by 50% when adding valproate.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Titrate lamotrigine slowly in the presence of valproate.

interactions.mechanism: Erythromycin inhibits CYP3A4, substantially increasing carbamazepine levels.
interactions.temporal_flag: Avoid combination; monitor carbamazepine levels if unavoidable.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Use azithromycin (1 dose) if antibiotic is required — shorter interaction window.

interactions.mechanism: No clinically significant pharmacokinetic interaction.
interactions.consequence: Potential additive immunosuppression; monitor for toxicity.
interactions.temporal_flag: Used together in triple DMARD therapy; monitor LFTs and CBC.
interactions.substitution_suggestion: Standard combination in RA; no dose adjustment required.
